holdings.note: SpaceX — IPO'd Jun 2026; FMP may not yet cover earnings/dividend dates for a brand-new listing.

macro.label: Triple witching
macro.detail: Quarterly simultaneous expiry of index futures, index options & single-stock options — volume/volatility spike and pinning risk in the most options-heavy names.

INTC.read: No dated company catalyst on the board for INTC — next earnings isn't in the feed, so there's no defined event window to trade around. Today's -3.95% on light relative volume (0.56) looks like flow/rotation rather than fundamental news, and with the position already down ~4.5% on the week, chasing weakness without a catalyst anchor is premature. The only scheduled item is triple witching on 2026-09-18 (~81 days out), which is a flow event, not a thesis trigger.
INTC.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (macro options expiry, no INTC-specific event scheduled)
INTC.bull: Light-volume drawdown with no news suggests forced/rotational selling that typically mean-reverts once the flow clears.
INTC.bear: No dated earnings catalyst to mark a bottom, and a -4.57% weekly slide can extend if sector rotation persists into Sept opex positioning.

NVDA.read: NVDA is down 8.75% on the week and 2% today with no company-specific dated catalyst on the board — no confirmed earnings or ex-div in the window. The drawdown is meaningful but without a defined event to mark the window, this is a tape-and-positioning read, not a catalyst read. Triple witching on 2026-09-18 is 81 days out and a market-structure event, not an NVDA-specific one.
NVDA.bull: 8-share position is small enough to ride; weakness without a binary print is usually where quality megacap AI names get re-accumulated.
NVDA.bear: No dated company catalyst means the -8.75% week could extend on macro/positioning before any earnings-driven reset.

ORCL.read: ORCL is down 19% over five sessions with no dated company catalyst on the board — that's a momentum break without an event to anchor a re-rate. Without a confirmed earnings date or guidance event in the window, there's nothing to lean into; the only thing on the calendar is Sept 18 triple witching, which is a flow event, not an ORCL-specific catalyst. The actionable window opens when the next print is confirmed (typically mid-September for OCI/RPO update) — until then, let the tape find a base.
ORCL.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (macro flow, not ORCL-specific); next ORCL earnings date not yet confirmed
ORCL.bull: OCI backlog and RPO growth re-accelerate at the next print, validating the AI-infra capex story and reclaiming the broken trend.
ORCL.bear: A -19% five-day move with no catalyst suggests positioning unwind or numbers concern; without a dated event, knife-catching here has no defined payoff.

NOK.read: NOK just dropped 6.84% in a single session with no dated company catalyst on the calendar, which makes this a price-action read rather than an event read. Without a confirmed earnings date or ex-div in front of us, there's no defined window to lean into — the next real test will be the Q3 print (typically late October), and that's the catalyst that would justify adding into this weakness or stepping aside. Triple witching on 9/18 is calendar noise, not a NOK-specific driver.
NOK.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (macro only; no dated NOK catalyst on deck)
NOK.bull: A -6.84% one-day flush with no company-specific news often marks a sentiment washout in a slow-moving telecom name; stabilization above $13 sets up a base into the next earnings print.
NOK.bear: A single-day drop this size usually has a reason — if it's a guidance leak or sector readthrough, $13 won't hold and the next print becomes the confirmation, not the bottom.

NBIS.read: NBIS is down 6.3% on the day and 16.3% over the week with no company-specific dated catalyst on the calendar — that's a momentum unwind without a known event to mark it against. Without a confirmed earnings date or other binary print to anchor a window, the honest move is to let the tape stabilize and see whether a dated catalyst (next print, AI capex commentary, customer deal) gets posted before leaning either way. The only thing ahead is Sep 18 triple witching, which is a flow event, not a fundamental one.
NBIS.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (macro flow event; no company-specific catalyst on calendar)
NBIS.bull: A clean AI-infra growth name selling off without news can set up an add window once a dated catalyst reappears and the slide bases.
NBIS.bear: 16% drawdown in five sessions with no catalyst suggests something the tape knows that you don't — adding into that without an event is catching a falling knife.

KO.read: Tiny 5-share KO position is up 2.7% on the day and 4.1% on the week with no company-specific dated catalyst on the board — no confirmed earnings or ex-div in the feed to anchor an add/trim window. The only thing ahead is triple witching on Sept 18, which is a tape event, not a KO-specific one. Without a dated catalyst, this is a hold-and-wait; the next real window opens when an earnings date or ex-div lands on the calendar.
KO.bull: Defensive staple grinding higher with broad tape, ~4% week — momentum confirms while you wait for the next dated print or dividend.
KO.bear: No catalyst means no edge; a strong-dollar or staples-rotation reversal could quietly bleed the recent gain before any event resets the setup.

SPCX.read: SPCX is a freshly public name (June 2026 IPO) with no confirmed earnings or dividend dates in the feed yet — typical for a sub-6-month listing where aggregators haven't picked up the reporting cadence. With only 10 shares and no dated company catalyst, there's no defined add/trim window to lean into; the only thing on the calendar is Sep 18 triple witching, which is a flow event, not a fundamental one. Best posture is to wait for the first earnings date to get confirmed (or a lockup-expiry disclosure) before framing a real catalyst trade.
SPCX.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (macro flow event; no company-specific catalyst dated yet)
SPCX.bull: First post-IPO print, once dated, sets up a clean expectations-reset window in a scarce-supply name with huge retail interest.
SPCX.bear: Post-IPO lockup expiry (typically ~180 days, so around Dec 2026) is an undated overhang that can pressure the tape before any earnings catalyst lands.

disclaimer: Forward earnings dates are third-party aggregator estimates, not company-confirmed — confirm against the company's IR release (~2 weeks ahead). Dividends, macro prints and witching are scheduled/confirmed. Not investment advice.
